default_annotator.report_text:
FrameworkPOS scraping malware installed on some of the thin clients, FrameworkPOS scraping malware installed on thin clients

initializing PowerShell/WMI stages that downloaded and reflectively loaded Cobalt-Strike beacon with PowerShell extension directly into the memory Initializing PowerShell stages

initializing PowerShell/WMI stages that downloaded and reflectively loaded Cobalt-Strike beacon with PowerShell extension directly into the memory Initializing WMI stages

initializing PowerShell/WMI stages that downloaded and reflectively loaded Cobalt-Strike beacon with PowerShell extension directly into the memory Downloading a payload such as the Cobalt Strike beacon.

initializing PowerShell/WMI stages that downloaded and reflectively loaded Cobalt-Strike beacon with PowerShell extension directly into the memory Loading code (such as Cobalt Strike beacon) directly into memory without writing it to disk

initializing PowerShell/WMI stages that downloaded and reflectively loaded Cobalt-Strike beacon with PowerShell extension directly into the memory Using PowerShell extensions to load the Cobalt Strike beacon into memory

the Cobalt Strike beacon payload gives attackers full control over the infected system Using remote access tools such as the Cobalt Strike beacon to control the infected system

the ability to move laterally to other systems Using remote services to move laterally between systems

harvest user credentials

evading advanced EDR scanning techniques Techniques for evading detection by Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) systems.

one vector is executed through HTA files Using HTA (HTML Application) files to execute malicious code

that execute PowerShell scripts HTA files that execute PowerShell scripts

part of an embedded VBScript Use of embedded VBScript to execute malicious commands.

some of them are executed through WMI Using WMI to execute commands and scripts

base64 encoded script Using base64 encoding to obfuscate script content

that is either remotely injected into the existing 32 bit or into a newly created 32 bit PowerShell process (if the current PowerShell is 64bit) Remotely injected into the existing 32 bit or into a newly created 32 bit PowerShell process

The injected shellcode is a regular Metasploit downloader shellcode Injected shellcode is a regular Metasploit downloader shellcode

that traverses the PEB, resolves the function names by the standard ROR 13 hash, Using advanced techniques to reflexively load code into memory by hashing out function names

downloads the next stage shellcode directly into memory from the C2 Downloading the payload of the next stage directly into memory from the command and control servers (C2)

2 types

regular direct reflective loaded Cobalt Strike DLL beacon, usually XOR encoded Direct reflexive loading of a DLL, a technique often used to inject code into memory without touching the disk

regular direct reflective loaded Cobalt Strike DLL beacon, usually XOR encoded Using XOR encoding to obfuscate the beacon

shellcode backdoor beacon with PowerShell and Mimikatz functionality Using PowerShell to execute malicious commands and scripts

shellcode backdoor beacon with PowerShell and Mimikatz functionality Using Mimikatz to collect credentials from the memory of the LSASS process.

install "WindowsHelpAssistant" task in the task scheduler Using the Windows task scheduler to maintain persistence.

uses rundll32.exe with System privileges to execute and export function "workerInstance" Uses rundll32.exe with System privileges to execute and export function "workerInstance" from a downloaded binary DLL "Assistant32.dll"

the first one

The second type

similar command execution as part of the HKLM Run key Using Run registry keys to maintain persistence

The malware XOR's (xor 0xAA) the credit card information before exfiltration through DNS tunneling. Obfuscation of information (XOR) before exfiltration

The malware XOR's (xor 0xAA) the credit card information before exfiltration through DNS tunneling. Using DNS tunneling to exfiltrate data.
